title: Re The Angel in the Opening Sequence
display_title: Re The Angel in the Opening Sequence
wikitext:
[]: Re: The Angel in the Opening Sequence...             Quote Post by clockworkbutterfly » Mon Feb 25, 2008 1:32 am Hello. I'm sorry if this has been asked before. I did search the forum for it, but didn't find anything to support that it had. Here is my question: What is the image of the angel/seraph from the opening credits? What is it called? Where did it originate? I have seen it in a few places, and I doubt it is originally from Evangelion. Does anyone have any idea where this image's origins lie? Here is the same image from a very different site, from those who don't want to check the OP directly: http://www.deadmoose.com/~rlpittsjr/Pics/Symbol_of_Lucifer_Wallpaper.jpg Any help with this would be greatly appreciated. Thank you very much.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Feb 25, 2008 2:06 am Here's something: http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?p=135775 http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=1449 http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=618

[]:             Quote Post by clockworkbutterfly » Mon Feb 25, 2008 9:30 am Thanks. It seems like I didn't know key terms like "Tetramorph" or "Sammael." Searching for "Evangelion Opening Angel" doesn't really limit the posts all that much. Thanks again.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Feb 25, 2008 11:45 am Editing this to save scrolling. clockworkbutterfly was interested in source only.

[]:             Quote Post by clockworkbutterfly » Mon Feb 25, 2008 12:08 pm It doesn't really matter to me what precisely this symbolizes or how many "wings" it has, or who it's an image of, or what kind of angel. All I seek to know is if this image was created purposely for Eva or if it was taken directly from another religious source and not altered in any way, like the Tree of Life which appears next in the opening credits. Based on the links to earlier forum posts, it seems pretty clear--although not definite--to me that this image is merely an altered form of the six-winged angel (as others have posted pictures of), made purposely for the sake of Evangelion (hence the "head" and the "core"), but if anyone can find this exact symbol used blatantly outside of Evangelion, I'd love to hear about it.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Feb 25, 2008 12:19 pm clockworkbutterfly wrote: It doesn't really matter to me what precisely this symbolizes or how many "wings" it has, or who it's an image of, or what kind of angel. All I seek to know is if this image was created purposely for Eva or if it was taken directly from another religious source and not altered in any way,

[]:             Quote Post by clockworkbutterfly » Fri Feb 29, 2008 10:45 am Zugzwang wrote: There is no information on the source of this image in the English-speaking fandom.
